name: ci
context: Running tests in CI/CD pipelines — GitHub Actions, parallelization, test splitting, flaky test management, coverage gates
info: This node covers running test suites in continuous integration pipelines: configuring GitHub Actions workflows for [[testing/jest]], [[testing/vitest]], [[testing/pytest]], and [[testing/playwright]]; parallelizing tests across workers and matrix strategies; splitting test suites for faster feedback; managing flaky tests with retries and quarantine; enforcing coverage thresholds as merge gates; and caching dependencies for fast CI runs. Agents use this node when setting up or debugging test pipelines, optimizing CI runtime, or integrating test results with pull request checks.
body:
# Testing in CI/CD

CI pipelines run tests automatically on every push and pull request. A well-configured pipeline catches bugs before merge, enforces coverage standards, and gives fast feedback to developers.

## GitHub Actions: Basic Test Workflow

```yaml
# .github/workflows/test.yml
name: Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm

      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test -- --coverage --ci

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: coverage-report
          path: coverage/
```

## Framework-Specific CI Configurations

### Jest / Vitest

```yaml
- run: npx jest --ci --coverage --maxWorkers=2
# or
- run: npx vitest run --coverage --reporter=junit --outputFile=test-results.xml
```

Key flags for CI:
- `--ci` (Jest) — disables interactive snapshots, uses `--maxWorkers=2` by default
- `run` (Vitest) — runs once without watch mode
- `--maxWorkers=2` — limit parallelism to available CI CPU cores
- `--coverage` — generate coverage reports
- `--reporter=junit` — output JUnit XML for CI tools

### pytest

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'
          cache: pip
      - run: pip install -e ".[test]"
      - run: pytest --cov=src --cov-report=xml --junitxml=test-results.xml -n auto
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: test-results
          path: test-results.xml
```

Key flags: `-n auto` (pytest-xdist parallelism), `--cov` (coverage), `--junitxml` (CI-parseable output).

### Playwright

```yaml
jobs:
  e2e:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
      - run: npx playwright test --project=chromium
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: playwright-report
          path: playwright-report/
          retention-days: 7
```

Playwright in CI: install only needed browsers (`--with-deps chromium`), run headless (default), upload traces and reports as artifacts for debugging failures.

## Parallelization Strategies

### Matrix Strategy

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [18, 20, 22]
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test
```

### Test Splitting (Shard)

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        shard: [1, 2, 3, 4]
    steps:
      - run: npx jest --shard=${{ matrix.shard }}/4
      # or
      - run: npx vitest run --shard=${{ matrix.shard }}/4
      # or
      - run: npx playwright test --shard=${{ matrix.shard }}/4
```

Sharding splits tests across N parallel jobs. Each shard runs a portion of the suite. Combine with `merge-reports` to get unified coverage.

### pytest-xdist Parallel Workers

```yaml
- run: pytest -n auto  # auto-detect CPU count
- run: pytest -n 4     # explicit 4 workers
```

## Dependency Caching

```yaml
# Node.js
- uses: actions/setup-node@v4
  with:
    cache: npm  # or 'yarn' or 'pnpm'

# Python
- uses: actions/setup-python@v5
  with:
    cache: pip

# Playwright browsers
- uses: actions/cache@v4
  with:
    path: ~/.cache/ms-playwright
    key: playwright-${{ hashFiles('package-lock.json') }}
```

Caching cuts CI time by 30-60%. Always cache: `node_modules` (via setup-node), pip packages, Playwright browsers.

## Coverage Gates

Enforce minimum coverage on pull requests:

```yaml
# In jest.config.js
coverageThreshold: {
  global: {
    branches: 80,
    functions: 80,
    lines: 80,
    statements: 80,
  },
}
```

```toml
# In pyproject.toml
[tool.coverage.report]
fail_under = 80
```

```yaml
# GitHub Actions step
- name: Check coverage threshold
  run: |
    coverage=$(jq '.total.lines.pct' coverage/coverage-summary.json)
    if (( $(echo "$coverage < 80" | bc -l) )); then
      echo "Coverage $coverage% is below 80% threshold"
      exit 1
    fi
```

## Flaky Test Management

Flaky tests pass and fail non-deterministically. They erode trust in CI.

**Detection:**
- Run tests multiple times: `npx jest --repeat=3`, `pytest --count=5` (pytest-repeat)
- Track flaky rate over time with test analytics

**Mitigation:**
- **Retry**: `retries: 2` in playwright.config.ts, `--retries 2` for Vitest
- **Quarantine**: tag flaky tests (`@pytest.mark.flaky`, `test.skip`) and fix them in dedicated sprints
- **Root causes**: shared state, time-dependent assertions, race conditions, network calls in tests

**Prevention:**
- Use [[testing/mocking]] for external dependencies
- Avoid `sleep()` — use polling/waiting utilities instead
- Isolate test state — each test gets a clean environment
- Randomize test order to surface hidden dependencies (`pytest-randomly`, `--randomize` flag)

## Environment Variables for CI

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `CI=true` | Most frameworks detect CI mode (no watch, no color) |
| `GITHUB_ACTIONS=true` | Set automatically in GitHub Actions |
| `JEST_WORKER_ID` | Worker ID for parallel Jest tests |
| `VITEST_WORKER_ID` | Worker ID for parallel Vitest tests |
| `PYTEST_CURRENT_TEST` | Currently running pytest test |
| `PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH` | Custom browser install location |

sections.title: GitHub Actions test workflows
sections.body: The standard CI platform for open-source and many enterprise projects. Use `actions/checkout@v4` to get code, `actions/setup-node@v4` or `actions/setup-python@v5` for runtime, then run test commands. Upload coverage and test reports as artifacts with `actions/upload-artifact@v4`. Use `if: always()` on artifact upload to preserve reports even when tests fail. For [[testing/playwright]], install browsers in CI with `npx playwright install --with-deps`.

sections.title: Parallelization and test splitting
sections.body: Three strategies: **Matrix** runs the full suite across multiple Node/Python versions or OSes. **Sharding** splits the suite across N jobs — `--shard=1/4` runs the first quarter. [[testing/jest]], [[testing/vitest]], and [[testing/playwright]] all support `--shard`. **Worker parallelism** runs tests in parallel within one job — `--maxWorkers=2` for Jest, `-n auto` for pytest-xdist. Combine sharding (across jobs) with worker parallelism (within jobs) for maximum throughput.

sections.title: Dependency caching
sections.body: Cache dependencies to avoid re-downloading on every run. `actions/setup-node@v4` caches npm/yarn/pnpm automatically with `cache: npm`. `actions/setup-python@v5` caches pip with `cache: pip`. For Playwright browsers, use `actions/cache@v4` with `~/.cache/ms-playwright` keyed on package-lock.json hash. Caching typically reduces CI time by 30-60%. Always verify cache hits in workflow logs.

sections.title: Coverage thresholds and gates
sections.body: Enforce minimum code coverage as a merge gate. [[testing/jest]]: `coverageThreshold` in jest.config.js sets per-metric minimums. [[testing/vitest]]: `coverage.thresholds` in vitest.config.ts. [[testing/pytest]]: `fail_under` in `[tool.coverage.report]` in pyproject.toml. Typical thresholds: 80% lines, 80% branches. Coverage gates prevent merging PRs that reduce coverage below the bar. Set thresholds incrementally — don't jump from 40% to 80% overnight.

sections.title: Flaky test management
sections.body: Flaky tests pass and fail non-deterministically. Root causes: shared mutable state, time-dependent assertions, network calls, race conditions, test ordering dependencies. Detection: run tests with `--repeat` or `--count` flags, track flaky rate in CI analytics. Mitigation: retry failures (`retries: 2` in Playwright, `--retries` in Vitest), quarantine known flaky tests with markers. Prevention: mock external deps ([[testing/mocking]]), avoid sleep (use waitFor/polling), isolate test state, randomize order (pytest-randomly).

sections.title: Test reporting and artifacts
sections.body: Generate machine-readable reports for CI tools: `--reporter=junit` (Vitest), `--junitxml=results.xml` (pytest), `--reporters=junit` (Playwright). Upload with `actions/upload-artifact@v4`. Coverage reports: `--coverage --coverageReporters=lcov` (Jest/Vitest), `--cov-report=xml` (pytest). Many CI tools parse JUnit XML natively. For Playwright, upload the HTML report and trace files — they're invaluable for debugging failures in CI.

links.context: Playwright CI: browser install, --shard, trace artifacts
Mocking prevents flaky tests from external dependencies
Good test patterns reduce CI flakiness

related.note: Jest's --ci flag and --shard are designed for CI environments
Playwright requires browser installation in CI and generates rich debug artifacts
Mocking external dependencies is critical for deterministic CI tests
